gold_evidence_groups.description: Holmes remembers Irene Adler as the woman.

questions.question: Who was "the woman" whom Sherlock Holmes always remembered?

gold_evidence_groups.description: Wilson was lured away from the shop so criminals could work nearby.

questions.question: Why was Jabez Wilson paid unusually well to copy the Encyclopaedia Britannica?
questions.reference_answer: The copying job was a ruse to draw Jabez Wilson away while the thieves prepared their bank robbery.

gold_evidence_groups.description: Hosmer Angel vanished from Mary Sutherland's life.

questions.question: Who was Hosmer Angel really, and why did he disappear?
questions.reference_answer: Hosmer Angel was James Windibank in disguise, and he disappeared to keep Mary Sutherland from marrying.

gold_evidence_groups.description: The five orange pips were a threatening sign connected with K. K. K.

questions.question: What did the five orange pips represent, and why were they sent?
questions.reference_answer: They were warnings or death threats from the Ku Klux Klan.

gold_evidence_groups.description: Neville St. Clair's life as Hugh Boone is revealed as begging for money.

questions.question: Why did Neville St. Clair live part of his life as the beggar Hugh Boone?
questions.reference_answer: He disguised himself as Hugh Boone because begging earned him far more money than his respectable work.

gold_evidence_groups.description: The carbuncle was found in a goose and traced through the goose chain.

questions.question: How did the stolen blue carbuncle end up inside a Christmas goose?
questions.reference_answer: Ryder hid the jewel by feeding it to a goose, then lost track of the bird.

gold_evidence_groups.description: The speckled band refers to the fatal snake.

questions.question: What was the "speckled band" that caused Julia Stoner's death?
questions.reference_answer: It was a venomous snake used by Dr. Roylott.

gold_evidence_groups.description: The Red-Headed League job was a lure for criminal access.
Neville St. Clair's work as a beggar was driven by money.

questions.question: Which two cases involved suspiciously generous employment offers, and what hidden purpose did each offer serve?
questions.reference_answer: The Red-Headed League used pay to lure Wilson away for a bank plot, and Neville St. Clair's beggar identity was maintained because it was lucrative.
